articles.title: How 7 Days of Meditation Can Change Your Brain
articles.summary: Scientists found that doing meditation for just one week can change your brain and body in good ways. In a study, 20 healthy adults went to a special retreat for 7 days where they did meditation and other activities for about 33 hours. Before and after, researchers checked their brains with scans and their blood with tests. They saw that meditation helped the brain become more flexible, improved how the body uses energy, made the immune system stronger, and reduced pain. People who felt more magical or connected during meditation had bigger changes in their brains. This shows that thinking and relaxing can really help your health.

articles.title: Monkey DNA Helps Save Rare Monkeys
articles.summary: Scientists have done something amazing for the first time ever! They mapped the DNA of a monkey named Crinkle, who lives at Trentham Monkey Forest in Staffordshire, England. Crinkle is a Barbary macaque, a type of monkey that is endangered, meaning there are not many left in the wild. By studying her DNA, researchers can now help protect these monkeys from dangers like poaching and illegal trade. This DNA map acts like a 'master key' to understand where the monkeys come from and track them better. For example, they already used it to find that Crinkle's family comes from wild areas in Morocco. This work gives hope for saving these special monkeys and could change how we care for endangered animals forever.

articles.title: Astronauts See the Moon Up Close in a New Record-Breaking Trip
articles.summary: Astronauts on the Artemis II mission flew around the Moon and set a new record for how far people have traveled from Earth. They saw amazing views of the Moon, like its craters and mountains, and even watched an eclipse where the Moon blocked the Sun. The astronauts had trained for years to learn about the Moon and take photos. They used a spacecraft called Orion, which worked well during the trip. The mission helped show that the Moon is a place we can explore more in the future. The astronauts said the sights were hard to describe because they were so beautiful and strange.

articles.title: A Lost World of Ancient Animals Found in China
articles.summary: Scientists have discovered a special fossil site in China that shows animals were more complex earlier than we thought. They found fossils from over 539 million years ago, before a time called the Cambrian Period when many animals suddenly appeared. This new site, called the Jiangchuan Biota, has fossils of early relatives of starfish, worms, and even animals like humans. The discovery helps us understand how animal life started on Earth, showing that many groups were already around earlier than scientists believed. It's like finding a lost world that changes our ideas about evolution.

articles.title: The Perfect Recipe for Life on Earth
articles.summary: Scientists have found a special secret about how life started on Earth. They discovered that two important elements, phosphorus and nitrogen, must be in just the right amounts when a planet forms. Phosphorus helps make DNA and gives energy to cells, while nitrogen is needed for proteins that build cells. During Earth's formation 4.6 billion years ago, the oxygen level was perfect, so these elements stayed on the surface where life could use them. If there was too much or too little oxygen, phosphorus and nitrogen would get lost in the core or escape into space. This is called the Goldilocks zone, where conditions are just right. This discovery helps scientists look for life on other planets by checking if they have similar oxygen levels during their formation.

articles.title: A Strange Planet with a Mysterious Atmosphere
articles.summary: Scientists have discovered a weird planet called TOI-5205 b using the James Webb Space Telescope. This planet is as big as Jupiter but orbits a tiny star. When it passes in front of its star, it blocks some light, letting astronomers study its atmosphere. They found that the planet's air has fewer heavy elements than expected, which is surprising because planets usually have more heavy stuff. This makes it a 'forbidden' planet that doesn't fit current ideas about how planets form. The atmosphere also has gases like methane and hydrogen sulfide. Researchers think the heavy elements might be hidden deep inside the planet. This discovery helps us learn more about how giant planets grow around small stars.
